plan.title: Port triage:accept / reject / defer actions to TypeScript

narratives.Description: Port scripts/triage_actions.py to a TypeScript triage actions module, preserving the accept, reject, and defer transitions and the cache mutations they apply to candidate state. This story is independently buildable in its own packages/core/src/triage/actions subdirectory and ships a golden parity binary against the Python oracle.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create the packages/core/src/triage/actions module that ports the accept, reject, and defer handlers and their candidate-state cache mutations from triage_actions.py, with vitest unit tests in the module directory.
2. Add a packages/cli/src/triage-actions-parity.ts parity binary that runs the TS actions and the Python scripts/triage_actions.py oracle and asserts byte-identical output with cache off.
3. Verify the port with the new vitest tests and the parity binary while the Python module remains the oracle.
narratives.UserStory: As an operator deciding on candidates, I want triage accept, reject, and defer available in TypeScript, so that those transitions run on the TS engine with the same cache effects as the Python module.

narrative.Acceptance: Running triage:accept, triage:reject, or triage:defer on the TS port updates the candidate state in the cache the same way the Python module does.
Running triage:defer persists the defer reason and suppression window on the candidate, matching the Python module's recorded fields.
Running the triage-actions parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python triage_actions.py oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/triage/actions module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs scripts/triage_actions.py under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-3 framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1725 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1725: feat(ts-migration): port the triage:* family to TS (Wave 3)
